source.title: The Universe’s biggest black holes may be forged in violent mergers

primary_journal_reference.title: Gravitational-wave constraints on the pair-instability mass gap and nuclear burning in massive stars

source.summary: Reports evidence from GWTC-4 that the heaviest detected black holes may form through repeated mergers in dense stellar clusters, with a transition near the pair-instability mass gap.

urf_relevance.theme: hierarchical mergers, gravitational-wave populations, and mass-gap structure
urf_relevance.frontier_role: external astrophysical source for gravity/cosmology source-map coverage; no theoretical closure

required_before_promotion: verify the Nature Astronomy primary paper
extract exact statistical model and assumptions
map only source-level implications into gravity-source vocabulary
